name: Lamiña Waterfalls - circular from Barcenillas
name_original: Cascadas de Lamiña – circular desde Barcenillas
region_intro: Hiking around Ruente offers access to the lush green landscapes of the Cabuérniga Valley, largely integrated into the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. The region features diverse natural elements including the Saja River, ancient oak forests like Monte Aá, and unique natural springs. Hikers can explore river valleys, dramatic gorges, and high mountain plateaus, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Ruente The most popular hiking route is Lamiña Waterfalls - circular from Barcenillas, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through a beautiful beech forest to the cascades. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parque Natural Saja-Besaya – circular route via the Bridges Route, a moderate 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This trail explores the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, traversing areas like Monte Río de los Vados. Local hikers also love the La Fuentona de Ruente - circular by the Saja river, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the unique natural spring and along the Saja River, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Hiking around Ruente is defined by unique natural springs, ancient oak forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle riverside walks to more challenging routes within the natural park. The routes in Ruente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Ruente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ruente?
A: Ruente offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, from gentle riverside walks to more challenging paths within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ruente?
A: Yes, Ruente has several routes suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996901397/la-fuentona-de-ruente-circular-by-the-saja-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona de Ruente - circular by the Saja river&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively short and gentle trail, perfect for a leisurely outing. It leads through the area surrounding the unique natural spring and along the Saja River.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ruente?
A: Many trails in Ruente, especially those within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Ruente?
A: Yes, Ruente features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996739843/lamina-waterfalls-circular-from-barcenillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamiña Waterfalls - circular from Barcenillas&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a beautiful beech forest to the cascades. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996742086/parque-natural-saja-besaya-circular-route-via-the-bridges-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Saja-Besaya – circular route via the Bridges Route&lt;/a&gt;, exploring the natural park's varied terrain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Ruente?
A: Ruente is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the mystical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt;, a unique natural monument, or visit the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The area also boasts the ancient Monte Aá oak forest and the dramatic Hoz de Santa Lucía gorge.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on hikes near Ruente?
A: Yes, the Waterfalls of Barcenillas are a notable attraction. You can reach them via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996739843/lamina-waterfalls-circular-from-barcenillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamiña Waterfalls - circular from Barcenillas&lt;/a&gt; route, which leads through a beautiful beech forest to these serene cascades.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ruente?
A: The lush green landscapes of Ruente make it an appealing hiking destination throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn showcasing beautiful fall foliage. Summer is also popular, though some routes might be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, but trails can be wet or muddy, and higher elevations might experience snow.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Ruente?
A: Hiking trails in Ruente range from easy to difficult. You'll find gentle riverside walks, moderate paths through forests and valleys, and more challenging routes within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park that involve greater elevation changes. Overall, there are 6 easy, 11 moderate, and 5 difficult routes available.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Ruente?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural features, including unique springs like La Fuentona, ancient oak forests, and the serene river valleys. The variety of terrain and well-defined trails also receive positive feedback.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, beyond natural wonders, you can encounter historical elements. The medieval bridge with nine arches at La Fuentona de Ruente adds historical charm. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3392395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Esteban Hermitage (Monte Corona)&lt;/a&gt; is a frequent destination for those exploring forest tracks and seeking beautiful panoramic vistas.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Ruente?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points for hikes in Ruente and the surrounding Saja-Besaya Natural Park offer designated parking areas. It is advisable to check specific route details or local signage for the most accurate parking information.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Ruente using public transport?
A: While Ruente is a small village, public transport options to the area might be limited. It's generally recommended to have a car for convenient access to various trailheads, especially those deeper within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. Check local bus schedules for connections to Ruente from larger towns in Cantabria.
tips:
They are located next to the town of Barcenillas, belonging to the municipality of Ruente, a beautiful spot located within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park.

The waterfalls are located in the middle course of the Arroyo de Barcenillas (which flows into the Saja), a small channel of crystalline waters immersed in a beautiful beech forest.

After a relaxing walk we arrive where the waterfalls, the sound of the water, and the greenery of the vegetation invite us to shut up and enjoy.
